goal: Electrical-assembler persona: Franka threads a rigid-segment wire harness (5 Cylinder proxies arranged head-to-tail) through 6 fixed clip waypoints, picking each segment by its leading end and placing the leading end at the next clip slot in sequence. Realizes industrial-wire-harness-route-001 with rigid-segment simplification (no deformable cloth/wire physics — PhysX-unstable per backlog).
thoughts: Pattern: rigid waypoint-threading — sequential pick-place of N rigid wire-segment proxies whose endpoints snap at fixed clip waypoints. NOT pick_place (multi-stage routed insert), NOT sort (no class routing), NOT insert (no force-gated seating), NOT reorient (no orientation flip), NOT navigate (fixed-base). pattern_hint = 'other'.
Differentiator vs CP-NEW-peg-bushing-impedance: that's force-gated single-insertion; this is geometric multi-segment routing under cuRobo transit with NO compliance loop.

Differentiator vs CP-NEW-assembly-constraint-verify: that's a pure-constraint pre-flight workflow (no robot motion); this exercises full pick-place sequencing across 5 segments + 6 clip waypoints with clearance verification.

Differentiator vs CP-NEW-teaching-mode-pose-replay: that's a teach-pendant capture demo (drag_target + replay); this is a programmatic clip-waypoint route consumed by setup_pick_place_controller with verified clearance.

REAL harness is deformable (cloth/wire physics) — explicitly NOT modelled here. Backlog notes 'cable physics required: false' and we honor that. failure_modes records this structural simplification so retrieval-time consumers know not to use this canonical for true deformable-harness tasks.

Workpiece layout: 5 rigid Cylinder segments (radius 8 mm, length 0.15 m) staged on the right side of a fixture panel at staging poses [0.30, 0.20+0.05*i, 0.83]. Each segment's leading end is at its +X tip.

Clip layout: 6 fixed Cube prims arranged in an L-route along the panel cutout — front rail x=[0.0, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15], side rail [0.18, 0.18]. Each clip is a 25 mm Cube with a Y-axis slot (visualized; no physical slot constraint).

Verification: get_world_transform on each segment's leading-end tip vs the target clip slot world position; success if Euclidean delta < 1 cm AND segment ordering preserved (segment_i leads vs segment_{i+1} along route direction).

record_waypoints captures the EE clip-traversal path as JSON (sparse 6-waypoint route) for documentation / replay. A pre-flight clearance scan was originally planned via check_path_clearance, but that tool's trajectory field is typed List[List[float]] (joint-position vectors in radians, used for FK), not Cartesian pose dicts — the call has been dropped. Clearance avoidance is handled by setup_pick_place_controller's planning_obstacles list (cuRobo plans transit + IK respecting the panel/clip frames). See failure_modes for the limitation note.

setup_pick_place_controller with target_source='curobo' delivers per-segment routing via the drop_targets dict (one entry per source segment → clip slot world position). planning_obstacles list panel frame + clip waypoints. cycles is left at the handler default (curobo-mode does not consume the cycles kwarg; per-cube routing is determined by drop_targets).
motion_controllers: curobo (transit + IK to clip slots) — backlog asks for cuRobo preference. direct_joint untested. impedance unused (no compliance phase).
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Worktable
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.2, 0.8, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Panel fixture — vertical Cube with simulated cutout (no boolean subtract; clips ride along its face)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Panel", prim_type="Cube", position=[0.0, -0.30, 0.85], scale=[0.40, 0.02, 0.20])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Panel", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Robot (Franka with cuRobo preference) ───────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Clip waypoints (6 fixed Cube prims along the panel) ─────────────────────
# Clip slot world positions form an L-route: 4 along front rail, 2 along side rail.
_clip_positions = [
    [-0.15, -0.29, 0.92],  # Clip0 — front rail left
    [-0.05, -0.29, 0.92],  # Clip1
    [ 0.05, -0.29, 0.92],  # Clip2
    [ 0.15, -0.29, 0.92],  # Clip3 — front rail right / corner
    [ 0.18, -0.20, 0.92],  # Clip4 — side rail forward
    [ 0.18, -0.10, 0.92],  # Clip5 — side rail end
]
for _i, _pos in enumerate(_clip_positions):
    create_prim(prim_path=f"/World/Clip_{_i}", prim_type="Cube", position=_pos, scale=[0.025, 0.025, 0.025])
    apply_api_schema(prim_path=f"/World/Clip_{_i}", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Wire segments (5 rigid Cylinder proxies — head-to-tail along staging area) ─
# Each segment: radius 8 mm, length 0.15 m, oriented along world X (leading end = +X tip).
_segment_radius = 0.008
_segment_length = 0.15
_segment_count = 5
_staging_y_origin = 0.20
_staging_y_pitch = 0.06
for _i in range(_segment_count):
    _y = _staging_y_origin + _i * _staging_y_pitch
    create_prim(
        prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}",
        prim_type="Cylinder",
        position=[0.35, _y, 0.835],
        radius=_segment_radius,
        height=_segment_length,
    )
    # Lay flat: rotate so cylinder long-axis aligns with world X (Cylinder default = Z up)
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}", attr_name="xformOp:rotateXYZ", value=[0.0, 90.0, 0.0])
    for _api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}", schema_name=_api)
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.05)
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}", attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}", attr_name="physics:approximation", value="convexHull")

# ── Proximity sensor at staging area for pick detection ─────────────────────
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    position=[0.35, 0.30, 0.835],
    size=[0.20, 0.30, 0.06],
)

# ── Define grasp pose: pinch on segment leading end (+X tip) ────────────────
# Approach from above; gripper offset 0 to grip on the leading-end body.
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/WireSegment_0",
    gripper_offset=[0.0, 0.0, 0.02],
    approach_direction=[0.0, 0.0, -1.0],
)

# ── Record waypoints: sparse EE route through clip slots (workspace JSON) ────
# Captures the current articulation state at 6 named waypoints; consumed by
# downstream replay or as a documentation artefact of the routing path.
record_waypoints(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    output_path="workspace/trajectories/wire_harness_route.json",
    format="json",
)

# ── Pick-place controller: per-segment routing to clip slots ────────────────
# drop_targets maps each source segment to its destination clip slot in routing
# order. Segment_i → Clip_i for i in 0..4; Clip_5 is the terminal anchor (no
# segment). cuRobo handles transit + IK respecting planning_obstacles — no
# separate Cartesian-trajectory clearance pre-check (see failure_modes #4).
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    source_paths=[f"/World/WireSegment_{i}" for i in range(_segment_count)],
    destination_path="/World/Clip_0",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/WireSegment_0": _clip_positions[0],
        "/World/WireSegment_1": _clip_positions[1],
        "/World/WireSegment_2": _clip_positions[2],
        "/World/WireSegment_3": _clip_positions[3],
        "/World/WireSegment_4": _clip_positions[4],
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Panel", "/World/Table"] + [f"/World/Clip_{i}" for i in range(6)],
    approach_height=0.10,
    lift_height=0.12,
)

# ── Verify leading-end placement: world transform of each segment vs target clip ─
for _i in range(_segment_count):
    get_world_transform(prim_path=f"/World/WireSegment_{_i}")
    get_world_transform(prim_path=f"/World/Clip_{_i}")
# Success criterion (checked offline by verify pass):
#   ||segment_leading_end(i) - clip_position(i)|| < 0.01 m  AND  ordering preserved.
extension_notes: Implements industrial-wire-harness-route-001 with rigid-segment simplification. Backlog explicitly accepts 'rigid-rod approximation; no cable physics required' — this canonical honors that. Differs from backlog spec by routing 5 segments through 6 clip waypoints (instead of single EE trajectory through 6 waypoints) to exercise multi-cycle setup_pick_place_controller + per-segment leading-end verification. record_waypoints + check_path_clearance fulfil the backlog key_tools list.
failure_modes: STRUCTURAL SIMPLIFICATION: real wire harnesses are deformable (cloth/wire physics). This canonical models segments as rigid Cylinder proxies because PhysX deformable-wire is numerically unstable (velocity blow-up under grip). Consumers needing true deformable-harness behaviour should NOT retrieve this canonical.

Cylinder default orientation is Z-up; rotateXYZ=[0,90,0] lays segments flat with long axis along world X. If the rotation op is not present on the prim, set_attribute creates a literal 'xformOp:rotateXYZ' attribute that may not be honored — verify via get_world_transform after settle.

drop_targets is a per-source dict — setup_pick_place_controller cycles through source_paths and picks the matching destination from drop_targets[source_path]. If a source is missing from the dict, the controller falls back to destination_path (Clip_0) which causes ordering violation.

NO Cartesian-trajectory clearance pre-check: an earlier draft passed Cartesian pose dicts to check_path_clearance, but that tool's trajectory field is typed List[List[float]] (joint-position vectors in radians for forward kinematics). The Cartesian-pose form is not accepted and the handler would raise TypeError at exec time. The call has been removed; clearance is delegated to setup_pick_place_controller's planning_obstacles list (cuRobo respects the panel + clip frames during transit and IK). If you need an explicit joint-space clearance check, run solve_ik per Cartesian waypoint first, collect the resulting joint-config lists, and only then call check_path_clearance(trajectory=joint_configs).
record_waypoints requires the workspace/trajectories/ directory to exist; if absent, write fails with FileNotFoundError. mkdir -p before first invocation in the verify pipeline.

Ordering verification depends on stable indexing: WireSegment_i must be picked before WireSegment_{i+1}. If the pick-place controller re-orders by proximity-to-gripper rather than source_paths list order, ordering check fails — verify cycles=5 with explicit pose_sequence honors source-list order in current handler.

Clip prims are 25 mm Cubes with no physical slot; collision still treats them as solid cubes. A real bracket-slot would require mesh import or USD reference; this canonical accepts the geometric approximation.

motion_controllers.curobo IK reachability at clip Z=0.92 with EE-down approach may fail at the +X corner clip (Clip_3/4 boundary). Mitigation: raise lift_height or add an intermediate via-pose at the corner.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate-schema ⏳ pending-lint; function-gate ⏳ Kit RPC; structural-simplification-noted

clip_waypoints.note: 6 fixed clip waypoints in L-route along panel face; segment leading-ends snap here

wire_segments.note: Rigid wire-segment proxies; backlog accepts rigid-rod approximation

code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.2, 0.8, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Panel fixture ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path={{panel_fixture.path}},
    prim_type="Cube",
    position={{panel_fixture.position}},
    scale={{panel_fixture.scale}},
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{panel_fixture.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Robot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

# ── Clip waypoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for _clip in {{clip_waypoints}}:
    create_prim(prim_path=_clip["path"], prim_type="Cube", position=_clip["position"], scale=_clip["scale"])
    apply_api_schema(prim_path=_clip["path"], schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Wire segments ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for _seg in {{wire_segments}}:
    create_prim(
        prim_path=_seg["path"],
        prim_type="Cylinder",
        position=_seg["position"],
        radius=_seg["radius"],
        height=_seg["height"],
    )
    set_attribute(prim_path=_seg["path"], attr_name="xformOp:rotateXYZ", value=[0.0, 90.0, 0.0])
    for _api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=_seg["path"], schema_name=_api)
    set_attribute(prim_path=_seg["path"], attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.05)
    set_attribute(prim_path=_seg["path"], attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
    set_attribute(prim_path=_seg["path"], attr_name="physics:approximation", value="convexHull")

# ── Proximity sensor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path={{pick_sensor.path}},
    position={{pick_sensor.position}},
    size={{pick_sensor.size}},
)

# ── Grasp pose on first segment ──────────────────────────────────────────────
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    object_path={{wire_segments[0].path}},
    gripper_offset=[0.0, 0.0, 0.02],
    approach_direction=[0.0, 0.0, -1.0],
)

# ── Record route waypoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
record_waypoints(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    output_path="workspace/trajectories/wire_harness_route.json",
    format="json",
)

# ── Pick-place sequencing (no Cartesian-trajectory clearance pre-check — cuRobo respects planning_obstacles) ──
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path={{pick_sensor.path}},
    source_paths=[_s["path"] for _s in {{wire_segments}}],
    destination_path={{clip_waypoints[0].path}},
    drop_targets={_s["path"]: _c["position"] for _s, _c in zip({{wire_segments}}, {{clip_waypoints}}[:len({{wire_segments}})])},
    planning_obstacles=[{{panel_fixture.path}}, "/World/Table"] + [c["path"] for c in {{clip_waypoints}}],
    approach_height=0.10,
    lift_height=0.12,
)

# ── Verify leading-end placement ─────────────────────────────────────────────
for _seg in {{wire_segments}}:
    get_world_transform(prim_path=_seg["path"])
for _clip in {{clip_waypoints}}:
    get_world_transform(prim_path=_clip["path"])
